title: Loading Arms - Carbis Solutions
chunk: Loading Arms Top Loading Arms The vast majority of chemical loading takes place via the top of the tanker or rail car. With gravity taking care of most of the work, the top fill and bottom drain are simplistic but effective. Any special requirements can then be carried out by the loading arm, either to capture vapor or to meter the amount of product being dispensed. At Carbis Solutions, we serve the petroleum, chemical and food industries, providing loading/unloading systems for safe and efficient handling of all types of liquids. Learn More Bottom Loading Arms The safety of the loading arm operator is the prime advantage since they remain on the ground – not on top of the vehicle where falls are a common threat. Connections are made more quickly so overall loading time is reduced. Bottom loading systems also create less turbulence in the tank, reducing the danger of generating static electricity and can be easily adapted to recover vapors displaced during loading. Learn More PTFE/ECTFE and Hastelloy® Loading Arms Do you have an extremely hazardous product line? We have Loading Arms that can withstand the toughest chemicals! Learn More Static Grounding Systems Loading and unloading tank trucks or railcars with chemicals and powders creates static electricity. Unchecked, it can spark with energy exceeding ignition levels for gases, vapors, and dust. Static risks demand attention, strong planning and design to prevent facility damage or loss of life. That’s why the ignition of such atmospheres by static electricity cannot be ignored. This risk in the manual handling process needs robust planning, design, and implementation to avoid damage to your facility and even loss of life. chunk: BLOG Marine Ladders, Towers, and Loading Arms Increase productivity and fall prevention simultaneously using a variety of fall prevention equipment that can be customized for your specific marine loading and offloading needs. Safety is key when working on ships and barges because of the unique risks inherent in these jobs. Protect your workers by ensuring your marine fall prevention equipment is up-to-date. Marine Ladders are designed to withstand particularly demanding marine access applications. The marine-grade aluminum construction is sturdy enough to help prevent falls, even in hazardous conditions. Marine Ladders are lightweight, yet they’re exceedingly durable because they resist corrosion from weather, salt water and chemicals. Safety features include: • slip-resistant coating • aluminum angle cleats for traction • open truss construction to reduce weight and wind resistance • some have rollers on the dock side to easily conform to changes in the ship’s float • safety shoes with rubber tread improve ladder’s footing on many surfaces • ladder styles with handrails start 4-feet from the bottom Towers allow workers safe access to all levels, regardless of the shape or size of

All towers are made of aluminum, which is lightweight, extremely tough and resists the elements. Tower options: • hydraulic, crane or pneumatic-driven systems • slip-resistant, self-leveling stairs with open serrated design • can be adjusted to compensate for variations in tide and ship’s equipment Stair Towers are OSHA-compliant and consist of stairs, a landing, a midrailand top rail. Use towers for construction, elevated platforms and buildings. The issues with hoses are that not only are they heavy, they create tripping hazards and sometimes they leak or break, causing a production slow down. You can eliminate these hazards by using marine loading arms instead. The great thing about loading arms is that they’re powered and counter balanced for easy use. There’s no need for a worker to injure his/her back dragging around a heavy hose. Save time, money and manpower while adding safety with marine loading arms. They provide the strength and stability needed to get the job done. Loading arms can transfer at higher rates and pressures than hoses so they will even increase your company’s efficiency and productivity. chunk: Our Process SERVICES The Difference is in the Details For almost fifty years, we have been leading in designing and manufacturing custom solutions for loading racks across many of the world’s most successful companies because we took the time to hear their pain points and see their vision of a solution. Our secret has always been that our clients know what they need; we just have to listen. The process of turning a project into a solution involves several key steps that ensure both clarity and alignment between the client and Carbis. It begins with a detailed consultation where the client’s requirements are thoroughly understood. This typically includes discussions on project scope, specifications, timelines, and budget, which are vital for crafting an accurate proposal or design. Site Assessment Discuss Options Engineer Solution Manufacturing Training SiteAssessment Helping You Spot Areas That Most Need Your Attention. Loading Rack Safety Assessments offer in-depth analysis and insight into whether your equipment is still doing the job that you need it to do. The equipment that was installed to handle a specific process or loading operation is often less than ideal when servicing

title: our-process - Carbis Solutions
chunk: ob that you need it to do. The equipment that was installed to handle a specific process or loading operation is often less than ideal when servicing new vehicles or new product lines. Whether it is a transition to ISO tainers or an addition of a new line of chemicals, we can help you figure out how to bring your loading rack into line with your current needs. Whether you need a hazard analysis of a particular spot, or of your entire operation, our team of fall protection specialists can help you to get the most out of your loading rack equipment, increasing safety and throughput. We will be happy to work with you to spot the areas that most need your attention and help you put together a plan to optimize your operation. We are committed to being your partner as we work together to keep your people safer and your products flowing. DiscussOptions What Were You Hoping We Could Do for You? That question drives a great deal of our mission with our clients. Our goal is not only to satisfy the basic functionality of fall protection, safety, and throughput within the project scope but also to ensure that our clients walk away from each meeting feeling that their needs are met and even exc

By listening to all decision-makers in a process and applying our experience we can find solution options ideal for each client. In-House Engineering From Concept to Creation Once the project scope and specifications are clearly defined, the design or solution phase begins. Engineers and project managers work to create a design that meets the client’s needs, which may involve detailed planning, drawing, and reviewing. Our detailers create more precise and detailed solutions and perform virtual testing of components and assemblies. This allows us to identify and resolve potential issues before submitting the finalized drawings to the client for approval. Master Manufacturing A Stair is Born Once a client has sent back approved drawings, the next step is to transition from design to production. The finalized CAD drawings provide a comprehensive blueprint, including dimensions, materials, and assembly instructions. These drawings are then sent to the production team for review and preparation. In the production facility, the engin

Depending on the complexity of the solution, components may undergo processes such as machining, welding, or molding. Quality control checks are conducted throughout this process to ensure the product meets the client’s standards. Site Training Operators Education – How to Use Your New Tools Training your team on how to use the new equipment is crucial for ensuring safety, efficiency, and optimal performance. Proper training helps employees become familiar with the equipment’s features, functionality, and potential hazards, reducing the risk of accidents and injuries. Our team of Carbis trainers will work with your team or in a train-the-trainer environment where your employees can ask questions and receive immediate feedback in a controlled environment, making it easier to address any concerns or misconceptions. Once the design is finalized, we move on to precise manufacturing, ensuring top-notch quality. Ultimately, our goal is for everyone to go home safely each night. chunk: About Us With Locations All Over the Globe, Sam Carbis Solutions Group, LLC Truly is the World Leader in Fall Protection Equipment We began in 1930 when our founder, Samuel Carbis, designed the first aluminum fire ladder, revolutionizing fire departments around the world. Since that time we’ve remained exclusively dedicated to designing and building the equipment and structures that assure safe access and fall prevention for workers around the world As a result, we are the global leader in customized bulk loading access equipment and turnkey systems for truck, rail, and marine applications across all industries. We are the only company in North America that designs and manufactures both access equipment and loading arms, ensuring they work together without interference. chunk: Multi SpotTruck Platform PLATFORMS Accelerate Your Truck Loading Without Compromising Safety Time is an essence for all logistics companies, and never more so in industries handling chemicals and liquid food products. That’s why multi hatch trucks offer huge potential benefits around the speed of access and vehicle throughput. But multi hatch trucks are complex platforms and to achieve your competitive edge you can’t cut corners. Because if you do, you are exposing your company to all manner of safety risks, both for your business and for your employees or external operators working in your loading bays. What Were You Hoping We Could Do For You? Get In Touch Multiple Spots, Multiple Configurations In today’s transportation world, products may be moved in any number of vehicles, each requiring its own access points. By working with the solutions team at Carbis, you can design the best system for your unique application. Whether loading or sampling, we can design a solution to keep your process moving smoothly. chunk: TruckSpotting System VEHICLE ALIGNMENT Simple Solutions for Precision Spotting Align with pinpoint accuracy, reducing errors, and improving safety during vehicle positioning. The system is designed to aid the truck driver in spotting the truck in a manner that the alignment of the vehicle is within proper working tolerance of the platform, product handling equipment, and fall protection equipment. The centerline of the truck from the face of the platform and the location of the hatch opening along the length of the truck can be accurately projected on a client supplied monitor for the driver. The corrections of spotting can be made on the way into the location by following visual clues on the screen. chunk: Top Loading Arms LOADING ARMS Custom Solutions Integrated With Your Fall Prevention Equipment Let us design the best top loading arms for your application. The vast majority of chemical loading takes place via the top of the tanker or rail car. With gravity taking care of most of the work, the top fill and bottom drain are simplistic but effective. Any special requirements can then be carried out by the loading arm, either to capture vapor or to meter the amount of product being dispensed. At Carbis Solutions, we serve the petroleum, chemical and food industries, providing loading/unloading systems for safe and efficient handling of all types of liquids. What Were You Hoping We Could Do For You? Get In Touch Designed to Work Together Carbis engineers their loading systems with your process in mind. title: History - Carbis Solutions
chunk: Our History 1930 Samuel Carbis invents the aluminum fire ladder. He begins manufacturing his ladders in his home’s garage in Pennsylvania. Within the year, his company, the Aluminum Ladder Company, was born with the trademark Alco-Lite. 1951 Samuel Henry Carbis passed away and left the company in the care of his children. His daughter, Helen (Carbis) Cramer, and her husband, Darrel Cramer, took over the helm. 1959 The Aluminum Ladder Company moved from Pennsylvania to Florence, South Carolina, as the development of the interstate system offered a more convenient method for shipping the ladders. A plentiful labor market and easy access to transportation saw the company thrive. 1968 Darrel Cramer passes away, leaving his wife Helen and son Samuel to lead the company. 1971 The federal government opened OSHA to increase workplace safety. 1976 Samuel starts a sister company, Carbis Ladders and Fabrications, to expand the family ladder business and to offer collateral products for industrial clients. 1980 In the early 1980s, Carbis changed direction as industrial clients began seeking help keeping their workplaces safer. To meet the rising demand for custom fall protection equipment, Car

title: History - Carbis Solutions
chunk: direction as industrial clients began seeking help keeping their workplaces safer. To meet the rising demand for custom fall protection equipment, Carbis Ladders and Fabrications gave way to Carbis, Inc., which featured an expanded engineering department. 1983 A fire ladder redesign sets Alco-Lite ladders, still headed by Helen Cramer, ahead of the competition among the aluminum fire ladder manufacturers in the United States. 1996 Carbis, Inc. breaks ground on a second manufacturing facility in Darlington, South Carolina, as demand for bulk loading equipment increases. 1998 Carbis, Inc. breaks with distribution and becomes the sole source for its custom loading equipment. 2001 Helen Cramer passes away, leaving her son, Samuel, in charge of both companies. 2013 Carbis and Aluminum Ladder are integrated into Sam Carbis Solutions Group, which offers engineering, manufacturing, and installation across many industries worldwide. Samuel Cramer assumes the role of Chairman of the Board, and his daughter, Shawn Mizell, takes the reins as president. 2016 Carbis picks up Excel Loading Arms as their primary loading arm manufacturer. 2019 Sam Carbis Solutions Group, LLC. Acquires its longtime

chunk: White Papers & Case Studies White Papers Transloading – Part One WHITEPAPER It’s fortuitous to find an oil well and strike it rich, just think how good the Beverly Hillbillies had it. But if you have no way to bring your product to market or in the case of bubbling crude – the refinery – then it isn’t worth anything.
